name: Community Legal Services Of Mid-Florida, Inc. - Ocala Office
description_short: Community Legal Services provides no-cost legal assistance to low-income Floridians through in-person clinics and consultations covering family law, housing, financial matters, and consumer rights.
description_long:
Community Legal Services of Mid-Florida has been serving the region for 60 years, establishing itself as a cornerstone nonprofit legal aid organization. The organization operates multiple offices across Central and East Florida, including locations in Ocala, Daytona Beach, and Orlando, with a mission to enhance lives through legal support, assistance, and education for those who cannot afford private legal counsel.

The organization offers comprehensive no-cost legal services across multiple practice areas including family law (divorce, custody), housing and fair housing disputes, financial matters, food assistance navigation (SNAP), disaster relief support, expungement and sealing of records, violence protection orders, elder law, student and children's rights, and small business/nonprofit formation assistance. They conduct regular legal advice clinics throughout their service area, with scheduled events visible on their calendar for April 2026 covering family law and record sealing in Volusia and Orange counties.

Community Legal Services distinguishes itself through its deep regional roots, multi-county presence, and specialization in underserved populations including older adults, families in crisis, small business owners, and individuals with criminal records seeking second chances. Their clinic-based model allows them to serve high volumes of low-income clients efficiently while maintaining a focus on both legal assistance and community education.

This is a legitimate, established nonprofit legal aid provider appropriate for low-income Floridians needing legal help. The primary limitation is that their services are geographically restricted to specific Florida counties and their clinic model means services are appointment or scheduled-event based rather than walk-in or 24/7 available.
pros: Completely free legal services—no cost to clients regardless of case complexity
60-year organizational history demonstrates stability and deep community roots
Multi-county service area with offices in Ocala, Daytona Beach, and Orlando
Regular scheduled legal clinics across multiple counties for accessibility
Specialized clinics for high-need areas like criminal record sealing and family law
Services for vulnerable populations including older adults and children
Covers diverse legal areas from housing to small business formation
cons: Geographically limited to specific Florida counties—unavailable outside service area
Clinic-based model requires advance scheduling; not walk-in or immediate access
Website provides limited detail on eligibility thresholds or income requirements
No indication of average wait times or response times for legal consultations
Limited information about whether they handle appeals or ongoing representation versus initial consultation only
best_for: Low-income Florida residents in Ocala, Daytona Beach, Orlando, or surrounding counties needing family law assistance
Individuals seeking to seal or expunge criminal records in Central/East Florida
Homeowners and renters facing housing disputes or fair housing violations
Seniors and older adults facing legal issues related to elder care or exploitation
diagnosis: Community Legal Services is best for low-income Floridians in Ocala, Daytona Beach, Orlando, and surrounding counties who need free legal help across family, housing, financial, or criminal justice matters. The main caveat is geographic limitation to their service area and reliance on scheduled clinics rather than immediate access.
services: Family law consultations (divorce, custody, child support)
Housing and fair housing legal advice
Criminal record expungement and sealing clinics
Small business formation and nonprofit services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
